nameOverride.description: Override the name of the chart

fullnameOverride.description: Override the full name of the chart

priorityClassName.description: Priority class name for spark-history-server pods

create.description: Whether to create a service account for spark-history-server

annotations.description: Annotations to add to the service account

name.description: Name of the service account to use for spark-history-server. If not set and create is true, a name will be generated using the fullname template

podAnnotations.description: Annotations to add to the spark-history-server pods

podSecurityContext.description: Pod-level security context for spark-operator controller pods.

securityContext.description: Container-level security context for the history server Pod.

externalPort.description: External port for the spark-history-server service

internalPort.description: Internal port for the spark-history-server service

enabled.description: Whether to create an ingress resource for spark-history-server

annotations.description: Annotations to add to the spark-history-server ingress

enabled.description: Whether to enable TLS for the spark-history-server ingress

secretName.description: Name of the secret containing the TLS certificate for the spark-history-server ingress

enabled.description: Whether to enable cert-manager integration for automatic TLS certificate management for the spark-history-server ingress.

secretName.description: Name of the secret to be created by cert-manager to store the TLS certificate for the spark-history-server ingress

duration.description: Duration in days for the TLS certificate issued by cert-manager for the spark-history-server ingress

additionalDnsNames.description: Additional DNS names to include in the TLS certificate for the spark-history-server ingress

additionalIpAddresses.description: Additional IP addresses to include in the TLS certificate for the spark-history-server ingress

clusterIssuerName.description: Name of the ClusterIssuer to use for cert-manager to issue the TLS certificate for the spark-history-server ingress. If not set, the default ClusterIssuer will be used.

resources.description: Compute Resources required by history server container. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/

enabled.description: Whether to enable liveness probe for spark-history-server

initialDelaySeconds.description: Initial delay in seconds for the liveness probe of spark-history-server

timeoutSeconds.description: Timeout in seconds for the liveness probe of spark-history-server

failureThreshold.description: Failure threshold for the liveness probe of spark-history-server

periodSeconds.description: Period in seconds for the liveness probe of spark-history-server

enabled.description: Whether to enable readiness probe for spark-history-server

initialDelaySeconds.description: Initial delay in seconds for the readiness probe of spark-history-server

timeoutSeconds.description: Timeout in seconds for the readiness probe of spark-history-server

failureThreshold.description: Failure threshold for the readiness probe of spark-history-server

periodSeconds.description: Period in seconds for the readiness probe of spark-history-server

hostAliases.description: Host aliases to add to the spark-history-server pods

enabled.description: Whether to enable Kerberos authentication for spark-history-server

keytab.description: Keytab file for the Kerberos principal for spark-history-server

config.description: Kerberos configuration file content for spark-history-server

sparkProperties.description: Additional Spark properties to set for spark-history-server in the format of key=value pairs separated by newlines

annotations.description: Annotations to add to the init job secret for spark-history-server. By default, the secret will be annotated with Helm hook annotations to create it before the main deployment and delete it after the hook is completed.

enabled.description: Whether to create an init job to set up S3 credentials for spark-history-server

awsSigV4.description: AWS SigV4 configuration for S3 compatibility

priorityClassName.description: Priority class name for the S3 init job. If not set, the init job will use the same priority class as the main deployment.

initAnnotations.description: Annotations to add to the S3 init job. By default, the job will be annotated with Helm hook annotations to create it before the main deployment and delete it before creating a new one on upgrade.

resources.description: Compute Resources required by s3 init job container. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/

logDirectory.description: Log directory for spark-history-server. This should be a directory in the S3 bucket configured for spark-history-server to store the Spark event logs.

enabled.description: Whether to enable S3 integration for spark-history-server

endpoint.description: S3 endpoint for spark-history-server to connect to the S3-compatible storage

accesskey.description: Access key for S3 authentication for spark-history-server

secretkey.description: Secret key for S3 authentication for spark-history-server

jceksPassword.description: Password for the JCEKS file to store S3 credentials for spark-history-server

enabled.description: Whether to enable HDFS integration for spark-history-server

hdfs_site.description: Content of the hdfs-site.xml configuration file for spark-history-server to connect to HDFS. This should be a valid XML content with the necessary HDFS configuration properties.

core_site.description: Content of the core-site.xml configuration file for spark-history-server to connect to HDFS. This should be a valid XML content with the necessary HDFS configuration properties.

enabled.description: Whether to enable oauth2-proxy as an authentication proxy in front of spark-history-server
Whether to enable Gateway API support for the oauth2-proxy ingress. If enabled, an HTTPRoute will be created instead of an Ingress resource for oauth2-proxy.

hostnames.description: Hostnames for the oauth2-proxy Gateway API HTTPRoute. This should be set if gateway.enabled is true.

parentRefs.description: Parent references for the oauth2-proxy Gateway API HTTPRoute. This should be set if gateway.enabled is true and should reference the Gateway resource to attach the HTTPRoute to.

rules.description: Additional rules for the oauth2-proxy Gateway API HTTPRoute. This can be used to specify additional routing rules or match conditions for the HTTPRoute.

labels.description: Labels to add to the oauth2-proxy Gateway API HTTPRoute

enabled.description: Whether to enable TLS for the backend connection from the oauth2-proxy to spark-history-server when using Gateway API. If enabled, the HTTPRoute will be configured to use TLS for the backend connection.

caCertificateRefs.description: References to Kubernetes secrets containing CA certificates to trust for the backend TLS connection from the oauth2-proxy to spark-history-server when using Gateway API. This should be set if backendTLSPolicy.enabled is true and should reference secrets containing the necessary CA certificates for the backend TLS connection.

hostname.description: Hostname to use for SNI when connecting from the oauth2-proxy to spark-history-server with TLS when using Gateway API. This should be set if backendTLSPolicy.enabled is true and should match the hostname on the TLS certificate used by spark-history-server.

wellKnownCACertificates.description: Comma-separated list of well-known CA certificates to trust for the backend TLS connection from the oauth2-proxy to spark-history-server when using Gateway API. This can be used as an alternative to specifying custom CA certificates with caCertificateRefs if the necessary CA certificates are included in the list of well-known CAs supported by the Kubernetes cluster.

subjectAltNames.description: Subject alternative names to verify when connecting from the oauth2-proxy to spark-history-server with TLS when using Gateway API. This can be used as an additional verification step for the backend TLS connection in addition to trusting specific CA certificates.

enabled.description: Whether to create a separate HTTPRoute for handling redirects from the oauth2-proxy when using Gateway API. If enabled, a separate HTTPRoute will be created with the specified hostnames and parentRefs to handle redirects from the oauth2-proxy.

parentRefs.description: Parent references for the oauth2-proxy redirect HTTPRoute when using Gateway API. This should be set if redirectRoute.enabled is true and should reference the Gateway resource to attach the redirect HTTPRoute to.

hostnames.description: Hostnames for the oauth2-proxy redirect HTTPRoute when using Gateway API. This should be set if redirectRoute.enabled is true.

command.description: Command to run in the oauth2-proxy container. If not set, the default command from the container image will be used.

kubeVersion.description: Kubernetes version to use for rendering the oauth2-proxy manifests. This can be set to a specific Kubernetes version (e.g., 1.21) to render manifests compatible with that version, or left as null to use the default rendering behavior which may include features based on the Kubernetes version of the cluster where the chart is being installed.

annotations.description: Annotations to add to the oauth2-proxy deployment

clientID.description: OAuth2 client ID for oauth2-proxy authentication

clientSecret.description: OAuth2 client secret for oauth2-proxy authentication

cookieSecret.description: Cookie secret for oauth2-proxy. This should be a random string of 16, 24, or 32 characters to secure the cookies used by oauth2-proxy.

cookieName.description: Cookie name for oauth2-proxy. If not set, the default cookie name will be used.

google.description: Google provider configuration for oauth2-proxy. This should include the necessary configuration properties for using Google as the OAuth2 provider with oauth2-proxy, such as the hosted domain to restrict sign-ins to a specific Google Workspace domain.

configFile.description: Path to a custom configuration file for oauth2-proxy. If set, this file will be used as the configuration for oauth2-proxy instead of the configuration generated from the other config properties. This can be used to provide a completely custom configuration for oauth2-proxy if the provided config properties do not cover all use cases or if you want to manage the configuration separately.

enabled.description: Whether to enable alpha configuration options for oauth2-proxy. This can be used to enable additional configuration options that are not yet considered stable or may require a specific Kubernetes version to work properly.

annotations.description: Annotations to add to the oauth2-proxy deployment when alphaConfig is enabled

serverConfigData.description: Additional server configuration data for oauth2-proxy to be included in the configuration file when alphaConfig is enabled. This can be used to specify additional configuration options for oauth2-proxy that are not covered by the other config properties and are only included in the configuration when alphaConfig is enabled.

metricsConfigData.description: Additional metrics configuration data for oauth2-proxy to be included in the configuration file when alphaConfig is enabled. This can be used to specify additional configuration options for the metrics endpoint of oauth2-proxy that are not covered by the other config properties and are only included in the configuration when alphaConfig is enabled.

configData.description: Additional configuration data for oauth2-proxy to be included in the configuration file when alphaConfig is enabled. This can be used to specify additional configuration options for oauth2-proxy that are not covered by the other config properties and are only included in the configuration when alphaConfig is enabled.

configFile.description: Path to a custom configuration file for oauth2-proxy to be used when alphaConfig is enabled. If set, this file will be used as the configuration for oauth2-proxy instead of the configuration generated from the other config properties when alphaConfig is enabled. This can be used to provide a completely custom configuration for oauth2-proxy when alphaConfig is enabled if the provided config properties do not cover all use cases or if you want to manage the configuration separately.

existingConfig.description: Name of an existing Kubernetes secret containing the configuration for oauth2-proxy to use when alphaConfig is enabled. If set, this secret will be used as the source of the configuration for oauth2-proxy instead of the configuration generated from the other config properties when alphaConfig is enabled. This can be used to reference a pre-existing secret containing the oauth2-proxy configuration when alphaConfig is enabled if you want to manage the configuration separately or have a specific reason to use a pre-existing secret.

existingSecret.description: Name of an existing Kubernetes secret containing the configuration for oauth2-proxy to use when alphaConfig is enabled. This is similar to existingConfig and can be used as an alternative property name for referencing a pre-existing secret containing the oauth2-proxy configuration when alphaConfig is enabled.

extraArgs.description: Additional command-line arguments to pass to the oauth2-proxy container. This can be used to specify additional configuration options for oauth2-proxy that are not covered by the other config properties by passing them as command-line arguments.

extraEnv.description: Additional environment variables to set in the oauth2-proxy container. This can be used to specify additional configuration options for oauth2-proxy that are not covered by the other config properties by passing them as environment variables.

customLabels.description: Custom labels to add to the oauth2-proxy deployment and related resources. This can be used to add additional labels for organizational or selection purposes.

enabled.description: Whether to create a file containing the list of authenticated emails for oauth2-proxy. If enabled, a file will be created with the list of authenticated emails and mounted into the oauth2-proxy container, and the configuration will be set to use this file for authentication.

persistence.description: Persistence method for the authenticated emails file for oauth2-proxy. This can be set to 'configmap' to use a ConfigMap to store the file, or 'secret' to use a Secret to store the file. The choice of persistence method may depend on the sensitivity of the data and how you want to manage it in Kubernetes.

template.description: Template for the authenticated emails file content. This should be a Go template that generates the content of the file based on the list of authenticated emails. The template will be rendered with a variable containing the list of authenticated emails, and the output will be used as the content of the file.

restrictedUserAccessKey.description: Key in the values file that contains the list of authenticated emails for oauth2-proxy. This should be set if authenticatedEmailsFile.enabled is true and should reference a value in the values file that contains the list of authenticated emails to be included in the file for oauth2-proxy.

restricted_access.description: Whether to restrict access to the oauth2-proxy to only authenticated users. This can be set to 'true' to restrict access to only authenticated users, or 'false' to allow access to unauthenticated users as well. This should be set if authenticatedEmailsFile.enabled is true and should be set based on whether you want to restrict access to the oauth2-proxy to only authenticated users or allow access to unauthenticated users as well.

annotations.description: Annotations to add to the ConfigMap or Secret used to store the authenticated emails file for oauth2-proxy. This can be used to add additional annotations for organizational or management purposes.

type.description: Service type for the oauth2-proxy service. This can be set to 'ClusterIP', 'NodePort', or 'LoadBalancer' depending on how you want to expose the oauth2-proxy service within the Kubernetes cluster or externally.

portNumber.description: Port number for the oauth2-proxy service. This is the port that the service will listen on and should match the port that oauth2-proxy is configured to use for its HTTP server.

portName.description: Port name for the oauth2-proxy service. This is an optional name for the port and can be used for organizational or selection purposes.

appProtocol.description: Application protocol for the oauth2-proxy service port. This can be set to 'http', 'https', or other supported application protocols to indicate the protocol used by the service port.

annotations.description: Annotations to add to the oauth2-proxy service. This can be used to add additional annotations for organizational or management purposes.

externalTrafficPolicy.description: External traffic policy for the oauth2-proxy service. This can be set to 'Cluster' or 'Local' if the service type is 'LoadBalancer' or 'NodePort' to control how external traffic is routed to the pods.

internalTrafficPolicy.description: Internal traffic policy for the oauth2-proxy service. This can be set to 'Cluster' or 'Local' to control how internal traffic is routed to the pods.

targetPort.description: Target port for the oauth2-proxy service. This should match the port that the oauth2-proxy container is listening on and can be set to a specific port number or a named port.

enabled.description: Whether to enable dual-stack IP support for the oauth2-proxy service. If enabled, the service will be configured to support both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses.

ipFamilies.description: List of IP families to support for the oauth2-proxy service when dual-stack is enabled. This should be set if ipDualStack.enabled is true and should include the IP families that you want to support for the service (e.g., 'IPv4', 'IPv6').

ipFamilyPolicy.description: IP family policy for the oauth2-proxy service when dual-stack is enabled. This can be set to 'SingleStack', 'PreferDualStack', or 'RequireDualStack' to control how the service handles IP families when dual-stack is enabled.

trafficDistribution.description: Traffic distribution strategy for the oauth2-proxy service. This can be set to 'Weighted' to enable weighted traffic distribution to different subsets of pods, or left as an empty string for the default round-robin distribution.

enabled.description: Whether to create a service account for oauth2-proxy. If enabled, a service account will be created and used for the oauth2-proxy deployment.

name.description: Name of the service account to create for oauth2-proxy. This should be set if serviceAccount.enabled is true and should be a valid Kubernetes service account name.

automountServiceAccountToken.description: Whether to automount the service account token for the oauth2-proxy pods. This can be set to false if you do not want the service account token to be automatically mounted into the pods, which can be a security consideration if the token is not needed for the operation of oauth2-proxy.

annotations.description: Annotations to add to the service account for oauth2-proxy. This can be used to add additional annotations for organizational or management purposes.

imagePullSecrets.description: Image pull secrets to add to the service account for oauth2-proxy. This can be used to specify image pull secrets if the container image for oauth2-proxy is stored in a private registry that requires authentication.

create.description: Whether to create a NetworkPolicy for oauth2-proxy. If enabled, a NetworkPolicy will be created to control the network traffic to and from the oauth2-proxy pods.

ingress.description: Ingress rules for the NetworkPolicy for oauth2-proxy. This should be set if networkPolicy.create is true and should include the necessary rules to allow traffic to the oauth2-proxy pods as needed.

egress.description: Egress rules for the NetworkPolicy for oauth2-proxy. This can be used to specify egress rules to control the outbound traffic from the oauth2-proxy pods if networkPolicy.create is true.

fullnameOverride.description: Full name override for the oauth2-proxy resources. This can be used to specify a custom full name for the oauth2-proxy resources instead of the default generated name.

enabled.description: Whether to create an Ingress resource for oauth2-proxy. If enabled, an Ingress will be created to expose the oauth2-proxy service externally.

path.description: Path for the Ingress resource for oauth2-proxy. This is the path that will be used to route traffic to the oauth2-proxy service through the Ingress.

supportsPathType.description: Whether the Kubernetes cluster supports the pathType field in Ingress resources. If true, the Ingress will be created with the specified pathType. If false, the pathType will not be included in the Ingress resource for compatibility with older Kubernetes versions that do not support it.

pathType.description: Path type for the Ingress resource for oauth2-proxy. This can be set to 'Exact', 'Prefix', or 'ImplementationSpecific' depending on how you want the Ingress to match the specified path. This should be set based on the desired routing behavior for the Ingress and the capabilities of the Kubernetes cluster.

hosts.description: Hostnames for the Ingress resource for oauth2-proxy. This should be set if ingress.enabled is true and should include the hostnames that you want to use to access the oauth2-proxy service through the Ingress.

annotations.description: Annotations to add to the Ingress resource for oauth2-proxy. By default, the Ingress is annotated to be ignored by the gateway-api-converter if that is being used in the cluster. This can be used to add additional annotations for organizational or management purposes.

labels.description: Labels to add to the Ingress resource for oauth2-proxy. This can be used to add additional labels for organizational or selection purposes.

resources.description: Compute Resources required by status provisioner container. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/

resizePolicy.description: Resize policy for the oauth2-proxy deployment. This can be used to specify rules for how the deployment should be resized when scaling up or down, such as which pods to add or remove first based on certain criteria.

extraVolumes.description: Additional volumes to add to the oauth2-proxy deployment. This can be used to specify additional volumes that should be mounted into the oauth2-proxy pods for use in the configuration or operation of oauth2-proxy.

extraVolumeMounts.description: Additional volume mounts to add to the oauth2-proxy deployment. This should be used in conjunction with extraVolumes to specify how the additional volumes should be mounted into the oauth2-proxy pods.

extraContainers.description: Additional containers to add to the oauth2-proxy deployment.

extraInitContainers.description: Additional init containers to add to the oauth2-proxy deployment.

priorityClassName.description: Priority class name for the oauth2-proxy pods. This can be set to the name of a PriorityClass in the Kubernetes cluster to assign a priority to the oauth2-proxy pods, which can affect scheduling and eviction behavior.

enabled.description: Whether to add host aliases to the oauth2-proxy pods. If enabled, host aliases will be added to the pod spec for the oauth2-proxy deployment based on the provided host alias configuration.

tolerations.description: Taints to tolerate for the oauth2-proxy pods. This can be used to specify which taints the oauth2-proxy pods should be allowed to tolerate.

nodeSelector.description: Node selector for the oauth2-proxy pods. This can be used to specify which nodes the oauth2-proxy pods should be scheduled on based on node labels.

proxyVarsAsSecrets.description: Whether to store the OAuth2 client ID, client secret, and cookie secret as Kubernetes secrets and reference them in the oauth2-proxy configuration instead of including them directly in the configuration file. If enabled, these values will be stored in a Kubernetes secret and the oauth2-proxy configuration will be set to reference the secrets for these values. This can be used to enhance security by not including sensitive values directly in the configuration file and instead referencing them from Kubernetes secrets.

secretsAsFiles.description: Whether to mount the Kubernetes secrets containing the OAuth2 client ID, client secret, and cookie secret as files in the oauth2-proxy pods and reference them in the configuration file as file paths instead of environment variables. If enabled, the secrets will be mounted as files and the oauth2-proxy configuration will be set to reference the file paths for these values. This can be used as an alternative to proxyVarsAsSecrets for referencing sensitive values from Kubernetes secrets in a way that does not involve environment variables.

enabled.description: Whether to enable a liveness probe for the oauth2-proxy pods. If enabled, a liveness probe will be configured to check the health of the oauth2-proxy pods and restart them if they are not healthy.

initialDelaySeconds.description: Initial delay in seconds for the liveness probe for the oauth2-proxy pods. This is the time to wait after the container has started before starting to perform liveness checks.

timeoutSeconds.description: Timeout in seconds for the liveness probe for the oauth2-proxy pods. This is the time to wait for a response from the liveness check before considering it a failure.

enabled.description: Whether to enable a readiness probe for the oauth2-proxy pods. If enabled, a readiness probe will be configured to check when the oauth2-proxy pods are ready to receive traffic and only route traffic to them when they are ready.

initialDelaySeconds.description: Initial delay in seconds for the readiness probe for the oauth2-proxy pods. This is the time to wait after the container has started before starting to perform readiness checks.

timeoutSeconds.description: Timeout in seconds for the readiness probe for the oauth2-proxy pods. This is the time to wait for a response from the readiness check before considering it a failure.

periodSeconds.description: Period in seconds for the readiness probe for the oauth2-proxy pods. This is the time to wait between performing readiness checks after the initial delay.

successThreshold.description: Success threshold for the readiness probe for the oauth2-proxy pods. This is the number of consecutive successful checks required before considering the pod ready after it has been marked as not ready.

securityContext.description: Pod-level security context for the oauth2proxy container.

deploymentAnnotations.description: Annotations to add to the oauth2-proxy deployment. This can be used to add additional annotations for organizational or management purposes.

podAnnotations.description: Annotations to add to the oauth2-proxy pods.

podLabels.description: Labels to add to the oauth2-proxy pods.

replicas.description: Number of replicas for the oauth2-proxy deployment. This should be set based on the desired level of availability and load handling for the oauth2-proxy service.

revisionHistoryLimit.description: Revision history limit for the oauth2-proxy deployment. This is the number of old ReplicaSets to retain for rollback purposes when the deployment is updated. This should be set based on how many previous versions of the deployment you want to be able to roll back to if needed.

enableServiceLinks.description: Whether to enable the injection of environment variables containing service information for the services in the same namespace as the oauth2-proxy deployment. This can be set to true to enable this feature, which can be useful for certain applications that need to access service information through environment variables, but it can also add additional environment variables to the pods which may not be desired in all cases.

enabled.description: Whether to create a PodDisruptionBudget for the oauth2-proxy deployment. If enabled, a PodDisruptionBudget will be created to specify the minimum number of available pods for the oauth2-proxy deployment during voluntary disruptions.

minAvailable.description: Minimum number of available pods for the PodDisruptionBudget for the oauth2-proxy deployment. This can be set to an integer value to specify an absolute number of pods that must be available, or a string percentage (e.g., '50%') to specify a percentage of the total number of pods that must be available.

maxUnavailable.description: Maximum number of unavailable pods for the PodDisruptionBudget for the oauth2-proxy deployment. This can be set to an integer value to specify an absolute number of pods that can be unavailable, or a string percentage (e.g., '50%') to specify a percentage of the total number of pods that can be unavailable. This should be set based on the desired level of availability and tolerance for disruptions for the oauth2-proxy deployment.

unhealthyPodEvictionPolicy.description: Policy for handling eviction of unhealthy pods when the PodDisruptionBudget is in effect. This can be set to 'Evict' to allow eviction of unhealthy pods, or 'DoNotEvict' to prevent eviction of unhealthy pods. This should be set based on the desired behavior for handling unhealthy pods during disruptions for the oauth2-proxy deployment.

enabled.description: Whether to enable autoscaling for the oauth2-proxy deployment. If enabled, a HorizontalPodAutoscaler will be created to automatically scale the number of replicas for the oauth2-proxy deployment based on the specified metrics and thresholds.

minReplicas.description: Minimum number of replicas for the HorizontalPodAutoscaler for the oauth2-proxy deployment. This should be set if autoscaling.enabled is true and should specify the minimum number of replicas that the autoscaler can scale down to.

maxReplicas.description: Maximum number of replicas for the HorizontalPodAutoscaler for the oauth2-proxy deployment. This should be set if autoscaling.enabled is true and should specify the maximum number of replicas that the autoscaler can scale up to.

targetCPUUtilizationPercentage.description: Target CPU utilization percentage for the HorizontalPodAutoscaler for the oauth2-proxy deployment. This should be set if autoscaling.enabled is true and should specify the target average CPU utilization across the pods in the deployment that the autoscaler will use to determine when to scale up or down.

annotations.description: Annotations to add to the HorizontalPodAutoscaler for the oauth2-proxy deployment. This can be used to add additional annotations for organizational or management purposes.

behavior.description: Behavior configuration for the HorizontalPodAutoscaler for the oauth2-proxy

podSecurityContext.description: Pod-level security context for the oauth2-proxy Pod.

httpScheme.description: HTTP scheme to use for the backend connection from the oauth2-proxy to spark-history-server. This can be set to 'http' or 'https' depending on whether you want the oauth2-proxy to connect to spark-history-server over HTTP or HTTPS.

enabled.description: Whether to include an init container that waits for Redis to be available before starting the oauth2-proxy container. This can be useful if you are using Redis for session storage and want to ensure that Redis is available before oauth2-proxy starts.

repository.description: Container image repository for the wait-for-redis init container

tag.description: Container image tag for the wait-for-redis init container

pullPolicy.description: Image pull policy for the wait-for-redis init container

kubectlVersion.description: Kubernetes version to use for rendering the wait-for-redis init container manifest. This can be set to a specific Kubernetes version (e.g., 1.21) to render a manifest compatible with that version, or left as null to use the default rendering behavior which may include features based on the Kubernetes version of the cluster where the chart is being installed.

securityContext.description: Container-level security context for the oauth2-proxy Pod.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for the wait-for-redis init container to wait for Redis to become available before giving up and failing. This should be set based on the expected startup time for Redis and the desired behavior when Redis is not available within the timeout period.

resources.description: Compute Resources required by oauth2-proxy container. More info: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/manage-resources-containers/

enabled.description: Whether to create a file containing htpasswd credentials for oauth2-proxy. If enabled, a file will be created with the htpasswd credentials and mounted into the oauth2-proxy container, and the configuration will be set to use this file for authentication.

existingSecret.description: Name of an existing Kubernetes secret containing the htpasswd credentials for oauth2-proxy. If set, this secret will be used as the source of the htpasswd credentials for oauth2-proxy instead of creating a new file from the provided credentials. This can be used to reference a pre-existing secret containing the htpasswd credentials if you want to manage the credentials separately or have a specific reason to use a pre-existing secret.

entries.description: List of htpasswd entries to include in the htpasswd file for oauth2-proxy. Each entry should be in the format 'username:hashedpassword' where 'hashedpassword' is the result of hashing the password using a compatible hashing algorithm (e.g., bcrypt) for use with htpasswd. This should be set if htpasswdFile.enabled is true and should include the necessary credentials for authentication with oauth2-proxy using htpasswd.

type.description: Session storage type for oauth2-proxy. This can be set to 'cookie' to use cookie-based session storage, or 'redis' to use Redis for session storage. The choice of session storage type may depend on the desired scalability and performance characteristics for oauth2-proxy, as well as the complexity of the deployment and the need for external dependencies.

existingSecret.description: Name of an existing Kubernetes secret containing the Redis connection information for oauth2-proxy session storage. If set, this secret will be used as the source of the Redis connection information for oauth2-proxy instead of using the other Redis configuration properties. This can be used to reference a pre-existing secret containing the Redis connection information if you want to manage the connection information separately or have a specific reason to use a pre-existing secret.

password.description: Password for the Redis instance used for session storage. This should be set if the Redis instance requires authentication.

passwordKey.description: Key in the values file that contains the password for the Redis instance used for session storage. This should be set if the Redis instance requires authentication and should reference a value in the values file that contains the password for the Redis instance.

clientType.description: Client type for connecting to Redis when using Redis for session storage. This can be set to 'standalone' for a single Redis instance, 'cluster' for a Redis cluster, or 'sentinel' for a Redis Sentinel setup. The choice of client type should match the architecture of the Redis deployment you are using for session storage.

connectionUrl.description: Connection URL for the standalone Redis instance used for session storage. This should be set if sessionStorage.type is 'redis' and sessionStorage.redis.clientType is 'standalone', and should include the necessary connection information (e.g., 'redis://:password@hostname:port') for connecting to the Redis instance.

connectionUrls.description: List of connection URLs for the Redis cluster nodes used for session storage. This should be set if sessionStorage.type is 'redis' and sessionStorage.redis.clientType is 'cluster', and should include the necessary connection information (e.g., 'redis://:password@hostname:port') for connecting to each node in the Redis cluster.

existingSecret.description: Name of an existing Kubernetes secret containing the Redis Sentinel connection information for oauth2-proxy session storage. If set, this secret will be used as the source of the Redis Sentinel connection information for oauth2-proxy instead of using the other Redis Sentinel configuration properties. This can be used to reference a pre-existing secret containing the Redis Sentinel connection information if you want to manage the connection information separately or have a specific reason to use a pre-existing secret.

password.description: Password for the Redis Sentinel setup used for session storage. This should be set if the Redis Sentinel setup requires authentication.

passwordKey.description: Key in the values file that contains the password for the Redis Sentinel setup used for session storage. This should be set if the Redis Sentinel setup requires authentication and should reference a value in the values file that contains the password for the Redis Sentinel setup.

masterName.description: Name of the Redis master node in the Sentinel setup used for session storage. This should be set if sessionStorage.type is 'redis' and sessionStorage.redis.clientType is 'sentinel', and should match the name of the master node as configured in the Redis Sentinel setup.

connectionUrls.description: List of connection URLs for the Redis Sentinel nodes used for session storage. This should be set if sessionStorage.type is 'redis' and sessionStorage.redis.clientType is 'sentinel', and should include the necessary connection information (e.g., 'redis://:password@hostname:port') for connecting to each node in the Redis Sentinel setup.

enabled.description: Whether to deploy a Redis instance for use with oauth2-proxy session storage. If enabled, a Redis deployment will be created and configured for use with oauth2-proxy if sessionStorage.type is set to 'redis'. This can be used to easily deploy a Redis instance alongside oauth2-proxy for session storage without needing to manage an external Redis deployment.

checkDeprecation.description: Whether to check for deprecated values in the configuration and emit warnings during chart installation or upgrade. If enabled, the chart will check for any values that are marked as deprecated in the schema and will emit warnings if such values are found in the provided configuration. This can be useful for identifying and addressing deprecated configurations before they cause issues in future versions of the chart.

enabled.description: Whether to enable metrics for oauth2-proxy. If enabled, oauth2-proxy will be configured to expose metrics in a format that can be scraped by Prometheus, and a Service and ServiceMonitor (if using the Prometheus Operator) will be created to allow Prometheus to scrape the metrics from oauth2-proxy.

port.description: Port number for the oauth2-proxy metrics endpoint. This is the port that oauth2-proxy will listen on for exposing metrics and should be set to a valid port number that is not in use by other services in the cluster.

appProtocol.description: Application protocol for the oauth2-proxy metrics service port. This can be set to 'http', 'https', or other supported application protocols to indicate the protocol used by the service port for metrics.

enabled.description: Whether to create a ServiceMonitor for oauth2-proxy metrics when using the Prometheus Operator. If enabled, a ServiceMonitor will be created to allow Prometheus to scrape the metrics from oauth2-proxy. This should be set if metrics.enabled is true and you are using the Prometheus Operator in your cluster to manage Prometheus instances.

namespace.description: Namespace to create the ServiceMonitor for oauth2-proxy metrics. This should be set if metrics.serviceMonitor.enabled is true and should specify the namespace where you want the ServiceMonitor to be created. If left as an empty string, the ServiceMonitor will be created in the same namespace as the oauth2-proxy deployment.

prometheusInstance.description: Name of the Prometheus instance to target with the ServiceMonitor for oauth2-proxy metrics. This should be set if metrics.serviceMonitor.enabled is true and you have multiple Prometheus instances in your cluster, and should match the name of the Prometheus instance as configured in the Prometheus Operator.

interval.description: Scrape interval for the ServiceMonitor for oauth2-proxy metrics. This should be set if metrics.serviceMonitor.enabled is true and should specify how frequently Prometheus should scrape the metrics from oauth2-proxy. The value should be a valid duration string (e.g., '30s', '1m') that indicates the desired scrape interval.

scrapeTimeout.description: Scrape timeout for the ServiceMonitor for oauth2-proxy metrics. This should be set if metrics.serviceMonitor.enabled is true and should specify the timeout for scraping the metrics from oauth2-proxy. The value should be a valid duration string (e.g., '15s', '30s') that indicates the desired scrape timeout.

labels.description: Labels to add to the ServiceMonitor for oauth2-proxy metrics. This can be used to add additional labels for organizational or selection purposes.

scheme.description: Scheme to use for scraping the oauth2-proxy metrics when using the ServiceMonitor. This can be set to 'http' or 'https' depending on how the oauth2-proxy metrics endpoint is configured and whether you want Prometheus to scrape the metrics over HTTP or HTTPS.

tlsConfig.description: TLS configuration for scraping the oauth2-proxy metrics when using the ServiceMonitor. This can be used to specify TLS settings such as CA certificates, client certificates, and insecure skip verify for scraping the metrics from oauth2-proxy over HTTPS.

bearerTokenFile.description: Path to a file containing a bearer token for authentication when scraping the oauth2-proxy metrics with the ServiceMonitor. This can be set if you want to use bearer token authentication for scraping the metrics from oauth2-proxy and should specify the path to a file that contains the bearer token to use for authentication.

annotations.description: Annotations to add to the ServiceMonitor for oauth2-proxy metrics. This can be used to add additional annotations for organizational or management purposes.

metricRelabelings.description: Metric relabeling rules for the ServiceMonitor for oauth2-proxy metrics. This can be used to specify relabeling rules to apply to the metrics from oauth2-proxy when they are scraped by Prometheus, such as to rename metrics, change label values, or drop certain metrics based on specified criteria.

relabelings.description: Relabeling rules for the ServiceMonitor for oauth2-proxy metrics. This can be used to specify relabeling rules to apply to the targets for scraping the metrics from oauth2-proxy when they are scraped by Prometheus, such as to change label values or drop certain targets based on specified criteria.

extraObjects.description: Additional Kubernetes objects to create for oauth2-proxy. This can be used to specify any additional Kubernetes resources that should be created alongside the default resources for oauth2-proxy, such as additional Services, ConfigMaps, or custom resources needed for specific configurations or integrations.

enabled.description: Whether to create a Gateway API Gateway resource for oauth2-proxy. If enabled, a Gateway will be created to expose the oauth2-proxy service using the Gateway API. This should be set if you want to use the Gateway API to manage the external exposure of oauth2-proxy instead of using an Ingress resource.

hostnames.description: Hostnames for the Gateway API Gateway resource for oauth2-proxy. This should be set if gateway.enabled is true and should include the hostnames that you want to use to access the oauth2-proxy service through the Gateway.

parentRefs.description: Parent references for the Gateway API Gateway resource for oauth2-proxy. This should be set if gateway.enabled is true and should include the parent references (e.g., to a GatewayClass or other resources) needed to properly configure the Gateway resource for oauth2-proxy.

rules.description: Rules for the Gateway API Gateway resource for oauth2-proxy. This can be used to specify the routing rules for the Gateway resource to route traffic to the oauth2-proxy service based on specified criteria such as hostnames, paths, or other conditions.

labels.description: Labels to add to the Gateway API Gateway resource for oauth2-proxy. This can be used to add additional labels for organizational or selection purposes.

enabled.description: Whether to create a redirect route for the Gateway API Gateway resource for oauth2-proxy. If enabled, a redirect route will be created to redirect traffic from HTTP to HTTPS or to another specified target. This can be used to ensure that traffic to oauth2-proxy is properly secured or routed according to your requirements.

parentRefs.description: Parent references for the redirect route of the Gateway API Gateway resource for oauth2-proxy. This should be set if gateway.redirectRoute.enabled is true and should include the parent references (e.g., to a GatewayClass or other resources) needed to properly configure the redirect route for oauth2-proxy.

hostnames.description: Hostnames for the redirect route of the Gateway API Gateway resource for oauth2-proxy. This should be set if gateway.redirectRoute.enabled is true and should include the hostnames that you want to use for the redirect route to access the oauth2-proxy service through the Gateway.
